scope_boundary: Runtime adapter adversarial boundary probe only. The probe validates two synthetic complete boundary reviews and rejects twelve expected-invalid controls for confused-deputy parent mismatch, parent and lease authority ceiling overruns, scoped-approval mismatch, expired approval, sandbox escape path, secret materialization into model-visible context, missing rollback handle, missing effect receipt, missing audit refs, support-state promotion, and missing non-claim boundary. It does not execute a deployed adapter, production sandbox, approval service, secret-handle broker, policy engine, rollback service, revocation service, benchmark, or security audit.
transition_reason: The Runtime adapter adversarial boundary probe is useful because it makes external-effect parentage, authority ceilings, scoped approvals, sandbox paths, secret non-materialization, rollback handles, effect receipts, audit refs, support-state boundaries, and non-claims executable as finite review routes. It remains a deterministic synthetic record gate, not deployed runtime evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed adapter behavior, sandbox isolation, approval-service behavior, secret-handle safety, policy-enforcement correctness, rollback-service behavior, revocation propagation, security review, benchmark performance, runtime security, or the Runtime Adapters chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable adapter execution trace
rollback execution in a target service
live effect receipts and audit refs
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: parent authority ceiling overrun is rejected
lease authority ceiling overrun is rejected
secret materialized into model-visible context is rejected
support-state promotion from record shape is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: Runtime adapter adversarial boundary validator failure
confused-deputy, authority-ceiling, approval, sandbox, secret, rollback, receipt, audit, support-state, or non-claim control accepted as valid

probe described as deployed adapter, sandbox-isolation, approval-service, secret-handle, policy-enforcement, rollback-service, revocation, benchmark, runtime-security, security-review, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed runtime-adapter boundary claim after deployed or externally reviewable adapter execution, sandbox-isolation evidence, approval-service behavior, secret-handle mediation, policy-enforcement tests, rollback execution in target services, deployed revocation propagation, live effect receipts and audit refs, residual accounting, security review, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent runtime-adapter, sandbox, approval-service, rollback-service, policy-enforcement, security, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable adapter execution trace
non_claims: does not promote Runtime adapter adversarial boundary evidence above argument

does not prove deployed adapter behavior, sandbox isolation, approval-service behavior, secret-handle safety, policy-enforcement correctness, rollback-service behavior, revocation propagation, security review, benchmark performance, runtime security, model behavior, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Runtime Adapters chapter core claim
